title: Mouthwatering Greek Lamb Keftedes
description: These lamb keftedes are fatty and aerated with a kick of horseradish, caramelized to perfection and macrobiotic-friendly for an energizing dinner.
instructions: In a large bowl mix lamb, onion, garlic, parsley, oregano, cumin, salt, pepper, horseradish and water until just combined.
Shape the mixture into 12 balls about 3 cm in diameter, keeping them airy by not overworking the meat.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat and sear each ball until browned on all sides, about 2 minutes per side.
Transfer the browned keftedes to a baking sheet and brush the tops with melted butter to promote caramelization.
Bake in the preheated oven for 12 minutes or until the internal temperature reaches 70°C.
Remove from the oven and let the keftedes rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve with a side of tzatziki, fresh cucumber salad, and whole grain pita for a complete Greek meal.
